name: Aaron Craig
bio: Aaron Craig is an artist whose work draws on playful candy and fruit motifs. Their pieces explore bold graphic illustration with a distinctive aesthetic that blends whimsy and contemporary expression.
artistStatement: I seek to capture the sweet and surreal—where everyday flavours become visual poetry.

name: Kai Voss
bio: Kai Voss is a Berlin-based abstract minimalist whose work explores the emotional weight of color and space. Trained at the Kunstakademie Düsseldorf, Voss strips composition to its essence—large color fields in earth tones that invite contemplation. His pieces have been shown at Art Basel, the Venice Biennale, and major galleries across Europe. Collectors describe his work as meditative, with each canvas functioning as a portal to stillness.
artistStatement: I believe that less reveals more—that in the space between pigment and viewer, something essential emerges.

style.palette: Earth tones, muted ochre and sienna

name: Elara Sato
bio: Elara Sato creates moody atmospheric landscapes that blur the line between memory and place. Born in Kyoto and based in London, she draws on both Eastern and Western traditions to craft scenes of quiet drama—dark palettes punctuated by hints of light. Her work has appeared in Tate Modern, the Mori Art Museum, and private collections worldwide. Sato's process involves extensive travel and long exposure photography, which she translates into painterly digital compositions.
artistStatement: I chase the moment when light breaks through darkness—that fragile edge where hope meets melancholy.

style.palette: Dark palette with hints of light

name: Marcus Chen
bio: Marcus Chen is a Los Angeles artist whose bold geometric pop art has redefined street-meets-gallery aesthetics. His vibrant canvases fuse Mondrian's grid with contemporary consumer culture, creating works that feel both nostalgic and urgent. Chen has collaborated with major brands and exhibited at MOCA and the Broad. His pieces bring energy and playfulness to any space, appealing to collectors who want art that sparks conversation.
artistStatement: I want my work to feel like a sugar rush—immediate, bold, and impossible to ignore.

name: Ivy Delacroix
bio: Ivy Delacroix specializes in botanical nature art that celebrates the quiet beauty of flora. Based in the Pacific Northwest, she draws inspiration from forest walks and greenhouse visits, translating organic forms into soft greens and golds. Her work has been featured in botanical journals, design magazines, and galleries from Seattle to Paris. Delacroix's pieces bring a sense of calm and natural elegance to interiors.
artistStatement: Every leaf and petal holds a story—I try to capture that whisper before it fades.

name: Ren Nakamura
bio: Ren Nakamura practices a contemporary form of wabi-sabi, embracing imperfection and transience in his Japanese-inspired compositions. Based in Tokyo and New York, he works with muted tones, organic textures, and deliberate asymmetry. Nakamura's pieces have been acquired by the Museum of Modern Art and the National Museum of Modern Art, Tokyo. His work invites viewers to find beauty in the incomplete and the ephemeral.
artistStatement: Perfection is an illusion—true beauty lives in the crack, the stain, the unfinished edge.

name: Leo Strand
bio: Leo Strand captures the raw energy of street art and urban grit. His work features layered textures, stencil influences, and a palette drawn from concrete and spray paint. Based in Brooklyn, Strand has painted murals worldwide and transitioned to limited editions that bring street aesthetics into the home. His pieces appeal to collectors who want art with edge and authenticity—work that feels alive and unpolished.
artistStatement: The city writes itself on every surface—I'm just taking dictation.

style.palette: Raw urban tones, concrete and spray paint

name: Aurora Cross
bio: Aurora Cross is a concept artist whose work bridges science fiction and fine art. Based in Los Angeles, she creates atmospheric scenes that feel both futuristic and timeless—vast landscapes, architectural wonders, and moments of quiet drama under alien skies. Her pieces have been featured in art books, game studios, and galleries from San Francisco to Tokyo. Collectors describe her work as immersive, with each canvas inviting you to step into another world.
artistStatement: I paint the moments between—the breath before discovery, the silence after the storm.

style.subjects: Vast landscapes, architectural wonders, alien skies

name: Sienna Blake
bio: Sienna Blake documents the natural world with the precision of a naturalist and the soul of a painter. Based in Melbourne, she draws on Australia's unique flora and fauna—banksias, orchids, cockatoos at dusk—to create works that feel both scientific and lyrical. Her pieces have appeared in botanical journals, design publications, and galleries across Australia and Europe. Blake's work brings the wild indoors, inviting contemplation of the living world.
artistStatement: Every species holds a story. I try to honor that before it slips away.

style.subjects: Australian flora and fauna, banksias, orchids

name: Jasper Vale
bio: Jasper Vale creates worlds where the ordinary meets the magical. His whimsical characters and soft palettes evoke childhood wonder without sentimentality—creatures that feel both familiar and strange, scenes that suggest stories just beyond the frame. Based in Portland, Vale has illustrated books, designed products, and exhibited in galleries from Seattle to London. His work appeals to collectors who want art that sparks imagination.
artistStatement: I paint the world as it might be if we looked a little more gently.

name: Atlas Reed
bio: Atlas Reed captures the beauty of decay—rusted metal, fractured landscapes, and figures that emerge from the wreckage. His work draws on industrial aesthetics and post-apocalyptic imagination, creating pieces that feel both brutal and tender. Based in Detroit, Reed has exhibited in underground galleries and design studios worldwide. His pieces appeal to collectors who want art with edge and narrative depth.
artistStatement: Ruin has its own grammar. I learn it, one canvas at a time.

name: Violet Ash
bio: Violet Ash paints figures that exist between waking and dream—young women with distant gazes, surrounded by botanical motifs and soft light. Her work blends Eastern and Western influences, creating a visual language that feels both intimate and universal. Based in Brooklyn, Ash has built a devoted following through galleries and social media. Her pieces bring a sense of quiet romance to any space.
artistStatement: I paint the moment before the thought completes—that fragile in-between.

style.subjects: Feminine figures, botanical motifs, dreamlike scenes

name: Luna Stone
bio: Luna Stone finds poetry in the ordinary—a cup of coffee, a window, a quiet street. Her minimalist illustrations use flat color and simple shapes to capture moments of calm and connection. Based in Madrid, Stone has worked with major brands and exhibited in design galleries worldwide. Her pieces bring warmth and simplicity to modern interiors, appealing to collectors who value understatement.
artistStatement: Less is a kind of attention. I try to give it.

name: Orion Wilde
bio: Orion Wilde creates images that feel like memories of dreams—astronauts floating in empty streets, figures suspended in impossible spaces, each detail rendered with photographic precision. His work has been exhibited in galleries from Melbourne to New York and collected by fans of both fine art and science fiction. Wilde's pieces invite viewers to pause and question what they're seeing.
artistStatement: I paint the moment when reality bends—when gravity forgets and light remembers.

name: Nova Gray
bio: Nova Gray inhabits the space between sweet and sinister—her work features figures that seem both innocent and haunted, with a vintage doll aesthetic that feels both nostalgic and unsettling. Based in Indianapolis, Gray has built a devoted following for her dark whimsy. Her pieces have been featured in galleries and collected by those who appreciate beauty with an edge.
artistStatement: Pretty things can hold shadows. I try to show both.

style.subjects: Vintage dolls, haunted figures, gothic beauty

name: Cedar Lane
bio: Cedar Lane paints worlds on the edge of tomorrow—atmospheric landscapes where the familiar meets the alien, where light falls in ways that feel both natural and impossible. Based in Paris, Lane's work draws on European and American sci-fi traditions. His pieces have been featured in concept art publications and exhibited in galleries worldwide. Collectors describe his work as meditative and otherworldly.
artistStatement: I paint the silence before the future arrives.

name: River Cross
bio: River Cross channels the spirit of mid-century America—hot rods, pin-ups, and the golden age of illustration. His work blends lowbrow aesthetic with fine art sensibility, creating pieces that feel both nostalgic and fresh. Based in Detroit, Cross has exhibited in galleries from LA to London. His pieces appeal to collectors who want art with personality and a sense of humor.
artistStatement: The past had better colors. I'm just borrowing them.

name: Willow Reed
bio: Willow Reed creates illustrated worlds where nature and imagination overlap—forests that fold into patterns, animals that blend into landscapes, and colors that feel both natural and impossible. Based in Melbourne, Reed has illustrated children's books and exhibited in galleries worldwide. Her work brings a sense of wonder and playfulness to modern interiors.
artistStatement: Nature doesn't need to be realistic to feel true.

name: Vera Lane
bio: Vera Lane creates art that pops—bold shapes, graphic colors, and compositions that feel both designed and spontaneous. Her work draws on mid-century modern and contemporary pop culture, creating pieces that energize any space. Based in Chicago, Lane has worked with brands and exhibited in design galleries. Her pieces appeal to collectors who want art with immediate impact.
artistStatement: Color is a language. I try to speak it clearly.

name: Ember Holloway
bio: Ember Holloway is an artist whose work draws on ANDY KEHOE style. Their pieces explore the boundaries between tradition and contemporary expression, creating compositions that invite contemplation and connection.
artistStatement: I seek to capture the in-between—the moment before form settles, where possibility still breathes.

name: Kira Ashworth
bio: Kira Ashworth is an artist whose work draws on JAMES JEAN style. Their pieces explore the boundaries between tradition and contemporary expression, creating compositions that invite contemplation and connection.
artistStatement: I seek to capture the in-between—the moment before form settles, where possibility still breathes.
